title: [Skyship, Deep Chambers]
[Skyship Lift]
description: A heavy metal gate seals the elevator opening, its surface covered in thick, vivid silver-white symbols that look freshly painted despite the ship's age.  The platform beyond is polished white stone, immaculate except for a single crimson handprint smeared near the center.  Along the gate's frame, tiny glaesine studs glow faintly, brightening when the elevator aligns with a floor and dimming when it departs.

A single lantern hangs above the platform, suspended in a black iron cage and filled with ashen white light that never flickers.  A ring of burnt parchment scraps has been pressed into the floor's seams, trapped beneath a thin glaze of hardened resin.  When the elevator rises, loose ash lifts in a slow halo around your ankles, swirling like a small storm that refuses to settle.

The room is a metal square, with no discernable pathways in the bulkheads leading out.  A constant, droning whir of large machinery echoes from behind these bulkheads, but its exact source is unseen.  Pale silver-white runes are painted across the floor, all alight with a faint magical glow.  A series of lanterns hangs from the ceiling, casting crisscrossing shadows across the area.

The elevator floor is a thick metal grating, the gaps beneath revealing a hollow drop littered with dried debris and dark stains.  At the platform's edges, shallow trenches catch runoff and funnel it toward circular drains stamped with simple stick-figure pyrography.  When the elevator moves, a wet, sucking echo rises from below, as if the shaft itself breathes.

A long recess in the wall reveals a counterweight assembly: a stacked column of bone and stone bound in iron bands, rising and falling behind a grated screen.  The bones are mismatched, humanoid ribs alongside thicker animal femurs, each stained a dull, old brown as if soaked in smoke.  A single skull is fixed to the grate at eye level, its teeth painted vermillion like a warning.
